question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: doUpdateEntity()] Can an unprivileged user create a topic with both autoRenewAccount and customFees.feeCollectorAccountId pointing to the same victim staking account, causing doUpdateEntity() to call recordItem.addEntityId() twice for the same account, and if duplicate entity ID processing causes a double-update of the account's staking state, corrupt the pending reward calculation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: createTopic()] Can an unprivileged user create a topic with feeExemptKeyList containing keys that match the keys of high-value staking accounts, causing createTopic() to serialize those keys via FeeExemptKeyList.newBuilder().addAllKeys(transactionBody.getFeeExemptKeyListList()).build().toByteArray(), and if the fee exemption check uses key matching against staking accounts, allow the attacker to exempt themselves from fees that fund the staking reward pool?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: doUpdateEntity()] Can an unprivileged user create a topic with autoRenewAccount set to an account that has declineReward=false and is staking to a node, causing doUpdateEntity() to add that account to the entity ID set via recordItem.addEntityId(autoRenewAccountId), and if the entity ID set is used to determine which accounts receive staking reward payouts in the current transaction, cause an unintended staking reward payout that resets the account's stakePeriodStart prematurely?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: doUpdateEntity()] Can an unprivileged user create a topic with autoRenewAccount set to an account that is about to receive a large staking reward at the end of the current staking period, causing doUpdateEntity() to set autoRenewAccountId without validation, and if the entity state update triggered by entityListener.onEntity(entity) causes the account's balance to be recalculated, reduce the stake_total_start used for reward computation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: doUpdateEntity()] Can an unprivileged user create a topic with autoRenewAccount set to a staking account and autoRenewPeriod set to a negative or zero value, causing doUpdateEntity() to call entity.setAutoRenewPeriod() with an invalid value, and if the staking reward system uses autoRenewPeriod in its calculations, produce an arithmetic underflow that corrupts the pending reward amount?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: doUpdateEntity()] Can an unprivileged user create a topic with autoRenewAccount set to a staking account, causing doUpdateEntity() to set entity.setType(EntityType.TOPIC) on an entity object that also has autoRenewAccountId set, and if the SqlEntityListener's entity merge logic does not properly discriminate by entity type, overwrite the staking account's entity record with TOPIC-typed data, clearing its staking fields?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getEntity()] Can an unprivileged user craft a ConsensusCreateTopic transaction where the receipt's topicID is manipulated to return an EntityId that collides with an existing staking account's ID, causing getEntity() to return that account's EntityId, and if AbstractEntityCrudTransactionHandler.updateEntity() then processes it as a CREATE operation, overwrite the staking account's entity record and reset its staking reward state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: doUpdateEntity()] Can an unprivileged user create a topic with customFees containing a fixed fee denominated in a token with denominatingTokenId set to null (no token), causing updateCustomFee() to call recordItem.addEntityId(null) via recordItem.addEntityId(tokenId) where tokenId is null, and if null entity ID processing causes a NullPointerException that rolls back the transaction batch, prevent staking reward transfers from being committed in the same batch?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: doUpdateEntity()] Can an unprivileged user create a topic with autoRenewAccount set to an account that is staked to a node with a high reward rate, causing doUpdateEntity() to call entity.setAutoRenewAccountId(autoRenewAccountId.getId()), and if the entity_stake query that computes pending rewards joins on autoRenewAccountId instead of the staking account's own ID, attribute the staking rewards to the topic entity instead of the legitimate staker?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: createTopic()] Can an unprivileged user create a topic with a feeScheduleKey set to null (hasFeeScheduleKey() returns false), causing createTopic() to set feeScheduleKey to null in the Topic builder, and if the absence of a feeScheduleKey causes the topic's custom fees to be unenforceable, allow the attacker to submit messages to the topic without paying fees that would otherwise fund the staking reward pool?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: doUpdateEntity()] Can an unprivileged user create a topic with autoRenewAccount set to an account that has a pending staking reward, and by triggering doUpdateEntity() to call entityListener.onEntity(entity) with a TOPIC entity carrying that autoRenewAccountId, cause the onStakingRewardTransfer() handler in SqlEntityListener to incorrectly update the stakePeriodStart of the autoRenewAccount, forfeiting its pending reward?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: doUpdateEntity()] Can an unprivileged user create a topic with autoRenewAccount set to a staking account and then immediately delete the topic, causing doUpdateEntity() to set autoRenewAccountId and then the delete handler to clear it, creating a window where the staking account's entity state is inconsistent and the pending reward calculation uses stale data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: doUpdateEntity()] Can an unprivileged user create a topic with customFees containing multiple fixed fees each with a different feeCollectorAccountId, causing updateCustomFee() to call recordItem.addEntityId(collector) for each collector, and if the entity ID set is used to determine which accounts are eligible for staking reward payouts in the current transaction, cause unintended staking reward payouts for all fee collector accounts?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: doUpdateEntity()] Can an unprivileged user create a topic with autoRenewAccount set to an account that is staking to a node, causing doUpdateEntity() to call entity.setAutoRenewAccountId(autoRenewAccountId.getId()) without checking if the autoRenewAccount is the same as the topic creator, and if the staking reward system uses the autoRenewAccountId to determine the staking beneficiary, allow the attacker to redirect staking rewards from the autoRenewAccount to themselves?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: createTopic()] Can an unprivileged user create a topic with a submitKey set to null (hasSubmitKey() returns false), causing createTopic() to set submitKey to null in the Topic builder, and if the absence of a submitKey allows anyone to submit messages to the topic, enable the attacker to flood the topic with messages that generate fees paid to fee collectors, draining the staking reward pool?
